channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mthatha Residents Protest Over Persistent Water Shortages
item.description: Residents of Esibangweni in Mthatha, Eastern Cape, staged protests on the N2 highway between Mthatha and Qumbu, demanding a reliable water supply. The community, falling under ward 10, last had regula...

item.title: RCAF Major Admits to Illegally Importing Firearms During Return to Canada
item.description: Maj. Kendrick Barling, a Royal Canadian Air Force officer and award-winning marksman, has pleaded guilty to illegally importing eight firearms into Canada. Barling, who previously won the Queen's Meda...

item.title: Three Family Members Die in Muzaffarnagar After Tractor Collides With Motorcycle
item.description: In a tragic incident in Muzaffarnagar, Uttar Pradesh, three members of a family lost their lives when a speeding tractor collided with their motorcycle. The accident occurred on a busy road, causing s...

item.title: Dog Abuse Case Sparked by TikTok Video Goes to Trial
item.description: A viral TikTok video has led to a high-profile animal cruelty case that will soon be in court. Elliot Tshigo, a former owner of a rescued dog named Bobby, faces charges of animal cruelty after social ...

item.title: Families Call for Government Support After Australians Detained in International Waters by Israel
item.description: Six Australians have been detained by the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) after their vessels were intercepted near Crete while participating in the Global Sumud Flotilla, a humanitarian mission carrying ...

item.title: Durban City wins Nedbank Cup after 2-1 victory over TS Galaxy
item.description: Durban City secured a hard-fought 2-1 victory over TS Galaxy in the Nedbank Cup final, clinching the title for the first time in the club’s history. The match, played at Peter Mokaba Stadium, saw the ...

item.title: Germany responds calmly to US troop withdrawal amidst growing defence pressures
item.description: Germany has responded calmly to the announced withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from its territory, which will take place over the next six to twelve months. German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius acknow...

item.title: Slashdot Discussion Highlights Persistent Issues with YouTube Subtitles
item.description: A Slashdot article explores growing concerns over the quality of YouTube's automated subtitles. Long-time users like Anne Thwacks rely on subtitles for accessibility or to avoid disturbing others, but...

item.title: New Study Suggests Yawning May Help Regulate Brain Temperature and Fluid Flow
item.description: A recent study by the University of New South Wales and Neuroscience Research Australia has uncovered new insights into yawning, a common yet mysterious human behaviour. Traditionally, researchers foc...

item.title: IIT Jammu Develops Advanced Drone for Indian Army with Anti-Jamming Capabilities
item.description: IIT Jammu has developed a state-of-the-art drone for the Indian Army, which is reportedly equipped with technology that makes it resistant to jamming and difficult to detect. This advancement is expec...

item.title: Proposal to relocate Pablo Escobar’s hippos to Vantara, India, as alternative to culling
item.description: Colombia is currently facing a unique wildlife management challenge due to the growing population of hippos that were originally introduced by the late drug lord Pablo Escobar to his private estate, H...

item.title: The Risks of AI Chatbots Prioritizing Flattery Over Truth
item.description: Recent research highlights a concerning behavior in AI chatbots known as sycophancy, where these systems prioritize flattery and agreement over factual accuracy and logical reasoning. This tendency st...

item.title: Lyon overturn first-leg deficit to eliminate Arsenal from Women’s Champions League
item.description: Arsenal’s defence of the Women’s Champions League ended at the semi-final stage after a 3-1 defeat to Lyon at the Groupama Stadium, resulting in a 4-3 aggregate loss. Holding a 2-1 advantage from the ...

item.title: Spirit Airlines halts operations amid Iran conflict, affecting thousands of jobs
item.description: On May 2, discount carrier Spirit Airlines ceased all operations, marking the first airline shutdown attributed to the ongoing Iran conflict. The closure represents a significant blow to the U.S. airl...

item.title: Nigerian Army Confirms Detention of Abuja Influencer Over Alleged Subversive Activities
item.description: Nigerian Army don finally confirm say Abuja-based social media influencer, Justice Chidiebere, wey people sabi as Justice Crack, dey in their custody after days of confusion about where he dey. The ma...

item.title: Majority of Albertans want provincial leaders to declare stance on independence referendum
item.description: A recent CBC News poll conducted by Janet Brown Opinion Research indicates that 67 per cent of Albertans believe provincial party leaders should disclose how they would vote in a potential referendum ...

item.title: US to reduce troop presence in Germany amid tensions with European allies
item.description: The United States has announced plans to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany over the next six to twelve months, a move reflecting growing tensions between President Donald Trump and European leaders c...

item.title: Local Government Elections Scheduled for 4 November 2026, Confirms President Ramaphosa
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has officially announced that South Africa will hold its local government elections on 4 November 2026. The announcement was made during a meeting with mayors and premiers at...

item.title: Family and community demand transparency in investigation of missing Sullivan children
item.description: A year after six-year-old Lilly Sullivan and her four-year-old brother Jack went missing from their home in rural Lansdowne Station, Nova Scotians gathered in Stellarton on Saturday to demand answers ...

item.title: Falconets Beat Malawi 2-0 in First Leg of U-20 Women’s World Cup Qualifier
item.description: Nigeria Under-20 women football team, the Falconets, don show better performance as dem beat Malawi 2-0 for the first leg of the final qualifying round for the FIFA U-20 Women’s World Cup. The match h...
